meta.title: 11.5 Data Architectures
meta.description: Lambda architecture, Kappa architecture, data mesh, and choosing the right data architecture.

blocks.prompt: You need both real-time dashboards (sub-second) and historical analytics (complex queries over years of data). Do you use one system or two?

blocks.content:
## Lambda Architecture

Two parallel processing paths:
- **Batch layer:** Processes all historical data periodically (Spark, complete but slow)
- **Speed layer:** Processes real-time stream (Kafka Streams/Flink, fast but approximate)
- **Serving layer:** Merges results from both layers

**Pros:** Handles both real-time and historical. Batch corrects any speed-layer inaccuracies.
**Cons:** Two code paths to maintain. Complexity of merging results.

## Kappa Architecture

One processing path: everything is a stream. Historical processing = replay the event log.

- **Stream layer:** Processes all events (Kafka + Flink)
- **Replay:** Reprocess historical data by resetting consumer offset

**Pros:** One code path. Simpler. Event log is the source of truth.
**Cons:** Replaying years of data can be slow. Not all processing fits the streaming model.

## Data Mesh

Decentralized data architecture. Each domain team owns its data as a product.

**Principles:**
1. **Domain ownership:** Teams own their data end-to-end
2. **Data as a product:** Treat data with the same care as a customer-facing product
3. **Self-serve platform:** Central team provides tools, not data processing
4. **Federated governance:** Shared standards, decentralized execution

**When to use:** Large organizations (100+ engineers) with multiple data domains.

| Architecture | Best for | Complexity |
|---|---|---|
| Lambda | Mixed batch + real-time | High |
| Kappa | Stream-first systems | Medium |
| Data Mesh | Large organizations | High (organizational) |
| Simple ETL | Small data, periodic reporting | Low |

Lambda uses dual batch+streaming paths (powerful but complex). Kappa simplifies to streaming only (replay for reprocessing). Data mesh decentralizes data ownership to domain teams. Choose based on your real-time requirements, data volume, and organizational size.

blocks.prompt: Module 11.6 is the checkpoint — design a data pipeline for a real-time analytics platform.

reviewQuestions.question: How does Lambda architecture process data?
options: Through two parallel paths: batch and speed layers
reviewQuestions.explanation: Lambda architecture uses a batch layer (complete but slow) and a speed layer (fast but approximate) in parallel.
reviewQuestions.encourageCorrect: Exactly! The dual-path approach gives you both accuracy and real-time results.
reviewQuestions.encourageIncorrect: Lambda architecture uses two parallel paths: a batch layer for historical processing and a speed layer for real-time.

reviewQuestions.question: What is the key simplification Kappa architecture makes compared to Lambda?
options: It uses only a streaming path for everything
It eliminates the need for a database
reviewQuestions.explanation: Kappa simplifies by using only a streaming path — historical reprocessing is done by replaying the event log.
reviewQuestions.encourageCorrect: That is right! One processing path instead of two makes Kappa much simpler to maintain.
reviewQuestions.encourageIncorrect: Kappa simplifies to streaming only — replay the event log when you need to reprocess historical data.
